species: The human cytomegalovirus is a common virus that causes lifelong infection in humans.
cell_type: To investigate whether US10 can interact with the PLC at all, we transiently transfected US10 and tapasin and analyzed the interaction with ERp57 using near steady-state conditions (metabolic labeling for 3 hr; Figure 2E).
disease_state: Human leucocyte antigen class I (HLA-I) molecules play a central role for both NK and T-cell responses that prevent serious human cytomegalovirus (HCMV) disease.
sample_source: Furthermore, in HCMV-infected fibroblasts inhibition of overlapping US10 and US11 transcription had little effect on HLA-A, but induced HLA-B antigen presentation.
cell_line: To assess whether the PLC is involved in the US10-mediated delay of HLA-I maturation, HLA-I interaction with the PLC was analyzed (Figure 2C).
